name: Memoization Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
For every index of string S1, we have different options to match that index with string S2. We can think in terms of string matching paths, as in earlier problems:

Either the characters match already.
Or, if there is a ?, we can explicitly match a single character.
For a *, it can match zero or more characters.


As there is no uniformity in data, the only way is to try all possible ways, which means using recursion.
Steps to form the recursive solution

Compare positions in both strings
      Treat one string as the pattern (letters, '?' or '*') and the other as the text. Start from the end and move backwards.
  
Handle current characters

Characters match: move back in both strings.
'?' â matches exactly one character: move back in both.
'*' â 
        
Match empty sequence , move back in pattern.
Match one or more characters,move back in text.


Different letters: no match.


Combine results: If any option leads to a match, the strings match.
Base cases

Both strings finished : match.
Pattern finished but text left: no match.
Text finished but pattern left : match only if all remaining are '*'.



Steps to memoize a recursive solution:
If we draw the recursion tree, we will see that there are overlapping subproblems. In order to convert a recursive solution the following steps will be taken:
Create a dp array of size [n][m]. The size of S1 and S2 are n and m respectively, so the variable i will always lie between â0â and ân-1â and the variable j between â0â and âm-1â.We initialize the dp array to -1.Whenever we want to find the answer to particular parameters (say f(i,j)), we first check whether the answer is already calculated using the dp array(i.e dp[i][j]!= -1 ). If yes, simply return the value from the dp array.If not, then we are finding the answer for the given value for the first time, we will use the recursive relation as usual but before returning from the function, we will set dp[i][j] to the solution we get.

complexities.time: O(N*M) ,There are N*M states therefore at max âN*Mâ new problems will be solved.
complexities.space: O(N*M) + O(N+M) , We are using a recursion stack space(O(N+M)) and a 2D array ( O(N*M)).

name: Tabulation Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
In the recursive logic, we set the base case too if(i<0 ) and if(j<0) but we canât set the dp arrayâs index to -1. Therefore a hack for this issue is to shift every index by 1 towards the right.


First we initialise the dp array of size [n+1][m+1] as zero.Next, we set the base condition (keep in mind 1-based indexing), we set the top-left cell as âtrueâ, then we set the first columnâs value as âfalseâ; and for the first row, we will run isAllStars() for every cell value.Similarly, we will implement the recursive code by keeping in mind the shifting of indexes, therefore S1[i] will be converted to S1[i-1]. Same for S2.At last we will print dp[n][m] as our answer.

complexities.space: O(N*M) ,We are using an external array of size âN*Mâ. Stack Space is eliminated.

name: Space Optimization Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
If we closely look the relation,
dp[i][j] =  dp[i-1][j-1], dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] ||dp[i][j-1] 
We see that to calculate a value of a cell of the dp array, we need only the previous row values (say prev) and current rowâs previous columns values. So, we donât need to store an entire array. Hence we can space optimise it.
We take two rows âprevâ and âcurâ.We initialize it to the base condition. We first initialize the prev row. Its first value needs to be true. Rest all the values of the prev row meeds to be false.Moreover, the cur variable whenever declared should have its first cellâs value given by isAllStarts() function.Next, we implement the memoization logic. We replace dp[i-1] with prev and dp[i] by cur.After every inner loop execution, we set prev=cur, for the next iteration.At last, we return prev[m] as our answer.

complexities.space: O(M) ,We are using an external array of size âM+1â to store two rows.

code.python:
class Solution:
    # Helper function to check if all characters in S1[0...i-1] are '*'
    # This matters because '*' can match an empty sequence
    def isAllStars(self, S1, i):
        # Loop through the first i characters
        for j in range(1, i + 1):
            # If any character is NOT '*', return False
            if S1[j - 1] != '*':
                return False
        # If all are '*', return True
        return True

    # Main function to perform wildcard pattern matching
    def wildcardMatching(self, S1, S2):
        # Length of pattern
        n = len(S1)
        # Length of text
        m = len(S2)

        # prev[j] â stores result of matching pattern[0...i-1] with text[0...j-1] for previous row
        prev = [False] * (m + 1)
        # cur[j] â stores result for current row
        cur = [False] * (m + 1)

        # Base case: Empty pattern matches empty string
        prev[0] = True

        # Outer loop â iterate over each character in the pattern
        for i in range(1, n + 1):
            # For first column (j = 0), match is possible only if pattern so far is all '*'
            cur[0] = self.isAllStars(S1, i)

            # Inner loop â iterate over each character in the text
            for j in range(1, m + 1):
                pChar = S1[i - 1]  # Current pattern character
                tChar = S2[j - 1]  # Current text character

                # Case 1: Characters match OR '?' wildcard matches exactly one character
                if pChar == tChar or pChar == '?':
                    cur[j] = prev[j - 1]  # Take diagonal value

                # Case 2: '*' wildcard can match:
                #    - Zero characters â take value from prev[j]
                #    - One or more characters â take value from cur[j - 1]
                elif pChar == '*':
                    cur[j] = prev[j] or cur[j - 1]

                # Case 3: Characters don't match and not a wildcard
                else:
                    cur[j] = False

            # Move current row into prev for next iteration
            prev = cur[:]

        # Answer is stored in prev[m]
        return prev[m]


# Driver code to test
if __name__ == "__main__":
    sol = Solution()
    S1 = "ab*cd"       # Pattern containing wildcard '*'
    S2 = "abdefcd"     # Text to match

    # Call matching function and print result
    if sol.wildcardMatching(S1, S2):
        print("String S1 and S2 do match")
    else:
        print("String S1 and S2 do not match")
